channel.title: Insurtech Insights
channel.description: In-depth coverage of how artificial intelligence is reshaping insurance — from claims automation to underwriting intelligence.

item.title: Fraudulent claims cost U.S. insurers $80 billion annually — but AI detection vendors promise to claw back 30–40% of that loss. My review of a dozen platforms shows half of those claims are inflated by 15–25%. Here’s how the top four stack up in real deployments.
item.description: I’ve reviewed dozens of predictive AI fraud prevention deployments across P&C carriers over the past 24 months, from Tier-1 mutuals to MGAs writing $50M GWP....

item.title: Why underwriting automation keeps failing (and why genAI looks different)
item.description: 14% of commercial insurance underwriters still manually review 90% of submissions — and carriers aren’t growing fast enough to justify the cost. That’s not a...

item.title: Why Your AI Claims Chatbot Is Losing Customers (And How to Fix It)
item.description: In 2023, Lemonade reported that 36% of its claims were fully resolved through its AI-powered Maya chatbot without human intervention. That sounds...

item.description: In 2023, a Tier-1 P&C carrier spent $14 M building a real-time underwriting pipeline on Apache Kafka, only to scrap it six months later when the actuarial team...

item.title: At 48% of U.S. term life portfolios, automated medical underwriting tools are now the default, not the exception. We put the top platforms through a 90-day pilot for a $500M block of fully underwritten business. Here’s what worked, what didn’t, and where the hidden costs live.
